title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, argues that smart glasses could become the next major computing platform by leveraging on-device AI assistants for daily tasks, context-aware help, and real-time information without disrupting user attention. The discussion covers Meta's strategy with Ray-Ban glasses, competitive pressures from OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition and device-side processing.
writeup: Alex Kantrowitz interviews Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, on the case for smart glasses as the next major computing platform for AI assistants. The discussion covers concrete use cases (meetings, reminders, and fitness tracking), the role of on-device AI, and competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon. It also addresses privacy concerns around facial recognition in wearable devices.
